summary: Tested Tax Jurisdictions + Product Images features end-to-end. Backend: 22/22 new tests pass (1 skipped — public catalog gated by unset PUBLIC_API_KEY, by design). Variant regression also 14/14 pass — confirms previous PATCH variant_id stock-decrement bug is fixed. Frontend: /admin/tax, /admin/orders/new, /admin/products (Photos section + file input), /admin/customers (Default tax jurisdiction select with 'No tax' + active jurisdictions) all render and integrate correctly.

issues: The shadcn Dialog overlay appears semi-transparent — page table headers behind the dialog are faintly visible. Cosmetic only; no interaction impact.

verified_flows: GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/tax-jurisdictions: admin write-restricted (employee 403), employee can read, soft-delete sets active=false, PATCH replaces components and persists.
POST/PATCH /api/customers: default_tax_jurisdiction_id stored, changeable, and explicit null clears it.
POST /api/orders without tax_jurisdiction_id falls back to customer default; tax_components, tax_jurisdiction_id, tax_jurisdiction_name persist.
Composite Karnataka GST CGST 9% + SGST 9% on $200 → tax_components [{CGST,9,18},{SGST,9,18}], tax=36, total=236.
Override with another jurisdiction id (VAT 7%) on $200 → tax=14, total=214.
Explicit '' (empty string) on a customer-with-default → tax=0, tax_components=[], total=subtotal-trade_in-credit.
Trade-in reduces taxable base BEFORE tax: $200-$50=$150 taxable * 18% = 27 tax, total 177.
PATCH /api/orders/{id} without tax_jurisdiction_id: rescales tax to new taxable using existing jurisdiction ($300 * 18% = $54).
PATCH /api/orders/{id} with tax_jurisdiction_id='' clears tax (tax=0, tax_components=[], jurisdiction_id=null).
POST /api/pricing/preview: returns tax_components, tax, total; supports default fallback, '' override, and explicit id override.
POST /api/products with images[{data_url,is_primary}] persists; GET returns. PATCH replaces images list verbatim. Default empty list when omitted.
Frontend Tax page renders with 'New jurisdiction' button + jurisdictions label.
Frontend OrderNew header includes 'Tax jurisdiction' select.
Frontend Products dialog includes Photos section with input[type=file].
Frontend Customers dialog includes 'Default tax jurisdiction' select prepopulated with 'No tax' + active jurisdictions.
Variant regression suite (14/14): previous PATCH variant_id stock decrement bug is fixed and confirmed.
action_items: (Optional) On PATCH /api/orders/{id} rescale path, _resolve_tax_jurisdiction is bypassed and find_one omits active=True filter — a soft-deleted (active:false) jurisdiction will still keep applying tax on edits to historical orders. Likely intentional (preserve historical tax) but worth confirming the design.
(Optional) /api/public/products is unconfigured (PUBLIC_API_KEY empty in /app/backend/.env). Public-catalog image flow test was skipped; set PUBLIC_API_KEY for that flow to be exercised.
(Cosmetic) Customer New-customer dialog overlay appears semi-transparent on /admin/customers.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1900+ lines — long-term should be split into routers (auth.py, products.py, orders.py, taxes.py, customers.py, public.py) following FastAPI router pattern. Currently a single-file god module.

_resolve_tax_jurisdiction filters by active=True, but the PATCH /orders rescale branch (line ~1141) does a raw find_one without that filter. Inconsistent with create-order semantics. Acceptable if intentional (don't drop tax on historical orders if jurisdiction archived), but should be a documented invariant.
Pydantic ConfigDict(extra='ignore') on Product / Customer / TaxJurisdiction is correct for forward-compat; consistent across models.

Server stores image data_url verbatim with no max-length / size validation. A malicious / mistaken upload of multi-MB base64 strings would bloat the products document beyond 16MB Mongo limit. Recommend a server-side soft cap (e.g., 1MB per image, 10 images per product) as defence in depth. Frontend compression mitigates but does not enforce.
tax_components stored as list-of-dicts {label, rate, amount}; OrderTaxLine model present but not used in OrderOut typing strictly (List[OrderTaxLine] declared correctly). OK.

_compute_tax returns (components, total, name, id). When taxable<=0 or jurisdiction is None the code returns id=jurisdiction['id'] only when jurisdiction truthy — this means if taxable<=0 BUT jurisdiction was selected, jurisdiction_id is preserved while components & amount are 0. Reasonable.

success_rate.backend: 100% (22/22 new tax+image tests; 14/14 variants regression; 1 skip = unconfigured public API by design)
success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke + integration of all four UI surfaces (Tax page, OrderNew select, Products Photos dialog, Customer Default-Tax select)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Karnataka_GST_*, TEST_Alt5pct_*, TEST_ToDelete_* tax jurisdictions; TEST Tax Product, TEST Image Product, TEST Image Replace, TEST No Image products; TEST_TaxCust_*, TEST_NoTaxCust_* customers; multiple TEST orders. All TEST_-prefixed and idempotent (each run creates fresh records via uuid suffix).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend & frontend feature tests for tax jurisdictions and product images pass. Tests are idempotent — rerun pytest /app/backend/tests/ at any time. Public catalog image test skips unless PUBLIC_API_KEY is set in /app/backend/.env. No regression in variants suite.
